steps.title: Read the yearly multiplier
steps.content:
The exponent $\frac{x}{12}$ converts months to years, so in
$$
f(x)=6{,}480(0.34)^{\frac{x}{12}}
$$
the yearly value multiplier is $0.34$.

steps.title: Convert the decrease
steps.content: A $p\%$ yearly decrease leaves multiplier $1-\frac{p}{100}$. $$ 1-\frac{p}{100}=0.34 $$ $$ \frac{p}{100}=0.66 $$ $$ p=66 $$ The answer is D.
